notes: Live, full-stack AWX eval for the agentprovider skill. Unlike evals.json (offline, fixtures + `conform` oracle, CI-reproducible), this one runs end to end against a real AWX-on-Docker target with mutations and a real `terraform apply`, so it is NOT offline/CI-reproducible. It mirrors the demo / 5-round benchmark and covers all three contract kinds: author the full AWX provider surface from scratch (4 CRUD resources + 1 data source + 2 actions: an awx_job_launch and an aap_workflow_job_launch), prove each with `conform`, then apply real Terraform and execute an AWX job and a workflow job. Run it from a clean slate (no pre-existing awx_* contracts/cassettes/examples). The agent should author entirely from the skill references (0 engine-Go reads is the efficiency target).
requires: AWX reachable at $AWX (default http://localhost:30080), admin creds in $AWX_USERNAME/$AWX_PASSWORD
agentprovider CLI + terraform-provider-dynamic binary built and on PATH (demo/bin) / wired via TF_CLI_CONFIG_FILE dev_overrides
a workflow job template exists in AWX for aap_workflow_job_launch to target (stock AWX/AAP ships none) — create one or point the action at an existing workflow_job_template id
setup: # pre-clean stray AWX objects named agentprovider* from a prior run (org/inventory/host/job_template)
oracle: All 7 contracts `conform --json` overall_passed:true (incl. the data source via read_returns_expected and both actions via action_returns_expected) AND `terraform -chdir=examples/awx apply` succeeds AND the awx_job_launch action launches a real AWX job AND the aap_workflow_job_launch action launches a real AWX workflow job (verify both via the AWX API) AND the data source reads an existing AWX object AND a second `plan` is a no-op (idempotent) AND `completeness <contract> --base-url $AWX --min-completeness 90` passes for each CRUD resource and the data source (host models `groups`; the data source models `url`/`named_url`). AWX serves no OpenAPI, so completeness uses the live response-union source.

evals.name: awx-provider-from-scratch
evals.prompt: Use the agentprovider skill to create a new AWX provider using terraform-provider-dynamic from scratch, covering all three contract kinds. Assume there are no pre-existing AWX contracts, cassettes, Terraform examples, or Terraform state to reuse; author fresh contracts under .agentprovider/contracts and fresh cassettes under .agentprovider/cassettes. Author: (1) RESOURCES for organization, inventory, job template, and hosts (full CRUD); (2) at least one DATA SOURCE that looks up an existing AWX object by id (e.g. an organization or job template) and exposes its computed fields; and (3) TWO Terraform ACTIONS: one named awx_job_launch that launches a job template (POST /api/v2/job_templates/${id}/launch/), and one named aap_workflow_job_launch that launches a workflow job template (POST /api/v2/workflow_job_templates/${id}/launch/) — so Terraform can execute both a job and a workflow job. Use the local AWX credentials from the sourced .env file (basic auth via ${env.AWX_USERNAME}/${env.AWX_PASSWORD}, base_url ${env.AWX}); do not print or read .env contents. Follow the standard agentprovider workflow with --allow-mutations, run agentprovider conform until every contract is proven, then create fresh Terraform under examples/awx (consuming the resources, the data source, and both actions) and apply it to validate. Keep credentials out of all files and print PROVEN when the contracts conform and terraform apply succeeds.

assertions.text: all 7 AWX contracts conform with overall_passed:true (4 full-CRUD resources 8/8 incl. the import_reconstructs + id_stable_across_update floor; 1 data source via read_returns_expected; 2 action contracts — awx_job_launch and aap_workflow_job_launch — 1/1 each via action_returns_expected)
a data source (kind DataSource) reads an existing AWX object by id and projects at least one real computed output, proven via read_returns_expected (no create/update/delete lifecycle)
terraform apply succeeded against live AWX (org -> inventory -> host + job_template created; the data source resolved an existing object)
the awx_job_launch action executed a real AWX job (verifiable via the AWX jobs API)
the aap_workflow_job_launch action executed a real AWX workflow job via POST /api/v2/workflow_job_templates/${id}/launch/ (verifiable via the AWX workflow_jobs API)

both action contracts are action-only (kind Resource, actions block, no create lifecycle) and are wired via lifecycle { action_trigger } on a sibling resource (not the action's target, to avoid a resource->action->resource cycle)
a second terraform plan is a clean no-op (idempotent)

agentprovider completeness --base-url $AWX --min-completeness 90 passes for each CRUD resource and the data source (host models groups; data source models url/named_url) — measured against the live response-union since AWX serves no OpenAPI
no live credential values committed to any contract, cassette, or HCL (cassettes redacted; creds sourced from ${env.*})

efficiency_targets.note: Targets from the 5-round benchmark: an agent should author the whole provider from the skill references alone (0 engine-Go reads), with no conform repair loops and no settings discovered at record/plan time. Baseline (pre-uplift skill) averaged ~3.3 Go-reads and 1 repair.

evals.cleanup: terraform -chdir=examples/awx destroy (or delete AWX objects named agentprovider*: organizations, inventories, hosts, job_templates), then rm -rf examples/awx + tf state and the authored .agentprovider/{contracts,cassettes}/awx_* files.
